v0.15.15.6 — Nightly Build Pipeline
denial_reason:
Governed workflow rejected by reviewer agent.
Verdict: flag (confidence 82%)
Findings: PLAN.md status marker changed from 'in_progress' to 'pending' — CLAUDE.md explicitly forbids agents from changing <!-- status: ... --> markers (only ta draft apply may do so). In practice ta draft apply will correct this, but it's a protocol violation.; Heredoc indentation bug in publish-nightly 'Build nightly release body' step: the heredoc content is indented ~10 spaces relative to the run block, so the release body file will contain leading whitespace on every line. GitHub Markdown treats 4+ leading spaces as a code block, which will break heading rendering (## Nightly Build...) and list formatting in the published release notes.; cross is installed from git HEAD (cargo install cross --git https://github.com/cross-rs/cross) rather than a pinned released version. This makes Linux cross-compilation builds non-deterministic and could silently break if cross introduces a regression.; USAGE.md not updated. CLAUDE.md requires updating USAGE.md when user-facing behavior changes. Nightly binary availability is user-facing (users can now get pre-release builds without building from source); a brief 'Nightly builds' section should be added.; README install snippet only shows 'ta' binary extraction; ta-daemon is not mentioned. Users following the README nightly install path would get an incomplete install missing the daemon process.; Validation step expects 'ta-nightly-x86_64-pc-windows-msvc.zip' in REQUIRED assets, but the packaging step also produces an MSI. Both are present in artifacts/* so upload covers both — no functional gap, but the zip file is redundant for a platform that ships an MSI as the primary installer.

title: v0.15.16 — Windows Code Signing (EV Certificate + CI Integration
denial_reason:
Governed workflow rejected by reviewer agent.
Verdict: flag (confidence 88%)
Findings: SECURITY (low severity in CI): sign-windows.ps1 sets $ErrorActionPreference = 'Stop', which causes Write-Error calls inside the foreach loop to throw terminating errors and exit the script before the temp PFX cleanup block runs. Affected paths: artifact-not-found, signtool sign failure, signtool verify failure. The temp PFX (containing the private key and cert) is left on disk until the runner is destroyed. On GitHub Actions ephemeral runners this is acceptable, but violates defense-in-depth. Fix: wrap the signing loop and cleanup in try/finally.; DEAD CODE: $msiPattern is defined in the release.yml inline script but never used — artifact collection uses Get-ChildItem -Filter '*.msi' instead. The variable is harmless but misleading.; PLAN.md items 1 (EV cert procurement) and 8 (macOS notarization) remain open with notes — both are human-action or deferred items, correctly documented and not expected to be closed by this agent.; No Rust code changed; no test coverage gap. Script correctness verified: signtool sign/verify invocation, SHA-256 digest, RFC 3161 timestamp, graceful secret-absent skip, and exit-code propagation all look correct.

title: v0.15.17 — `ta doctor`: Auth Validation & Agent-Agnostic Auth Spec
v0.15.16 — Windows Code Signing (EV Certificate + CI Integration
denial_reason:
Governed workflow rejected by reviewer agent.
Verdict: flag (confidence 88%)
Findings: PLAN.md status marker regressed: the real project has `<!-- status: in_progress -->` for v0.15.16 but the staged PLAN.md changes it to `<!-- status: pending -->`. CLAUDE.md explicitly forbids agents from changing status markers — only `ta draft apply` may do so. This is both a constitution violation and a semantic regression (moving backwards from in_progress to pending).; Minor resource leak in sign-windows.ps1: `[System.IO.Path]::GetTempFileName()` creates a zero-byte file on disk, then `.pfx` is appended to produce a *different* path. The original zero-byte temp file is never cleaned up. Not a security issue, but the `finally` block only removes `$tempPfx` (the `.pfx` path). Recommend using `[System.IO.Path]::Combine([System.IO.Path]::GetTempPath(), [System.IO.Path]::GetRandomFileName() + '.pfx')` instead.; The cert password is passed via the signtool.exe argument array (`/p $certPassword`), which may expose it in process listings on some systems. This is a known limitation of signtool.exe with no clean workaround; GitHub Actions masks the secret in logs. Acceptable but worth noting.; Core deliverables (sign-windows.ps1 with try/finally PFX cleanup, release.yml signing step, docs/release-ops.md runbook) are correct and complete. The try/finally fix addresses the issue that caused the previous draft to be flagged. Items 1, 4, 8, 9 are correctly deferred as requiring human action or a live EV cert.

title: v0.15.18 — Project TA Version Tracking & Upgrade Path
denial_reason:
Governed workflow rejected by reviewer agent.
Verdict: flag (confidence 82%)
Findings: TOML corruption risk in add_pr_poll_interval: the function appends a bare '[config]' section to workflow.toml without checking whether a '[config]' section already exists. If a project has a [config] section without pr_poll_interval_secs, the result is invalid TOML (duplicate section). The check fn (workflow_has_pr_poll) only guards against running when the key is absent — it does not prevent appending a duplicate section header. A targeted fix would be to append only the key under the existing section rather than appending a full new section.; GoalRunState::Failed reused for intentional user-initiated closure (ta doctor --fix-denied): while the agent decision log explains the rationale (no Closed variant, reason string makes it clear), this leaks an impl shortcut into user-visible state. Future tooling filtering on 'failed' goals will include these intentionally-closed ones. Agent acknowledged the trade-off; flagging for human sign-off.; upgrade.rs: gitignore_check_idempotent test documents that calling append_gitignore twice produces duplicate entries, placing idempotency responsibility on the check function. The execute() flow correctly runs check before apply, so this is not a bug today. However, if --force bypasses the check fn (it does not — force only bypasses the version gate, check still runs), duplicates could occur. Verify --force path re-runs check before apply; inspection confirms check is always called, so this is a low-risk documentation note.; is_acknowledged uses substring matching (description.contains(pattern)): a pattern like 'a' would suppress all steps. Intended for power users who understand the format, but the --acknowledge UX does not warn about broad patterns. Low risk in practice.

v0.15.22 — Secret Scan: Real-Threat Discrimination
denial_reason:
Governed workflow rejected by reviewer agent.
Verdict: flag (confidence 82%)
Findings: PLAN.md status marker changed from 'in_progress' to 'pending' — CLAUDE.md explicitly forbids agents from changing <!-- status: ... --> markers (only ta draft apply may transition them). The marker should remain 'in_progress'; TA apply will set it to 'done'. Impact is low since apply auto-corrects, but the rule was violated.; is_doc_example() uses lower_val.contains(placeholder) for short generic words ('test', 'fake', 'dummy', 'sample', 'example'). For known-service tokens (Slack xoxb-*, GitHub ghp_*, etc.) the doc-example check runs BEFORE the known_service guard and always wins. A real Slack token whose 36-char payload happens to contain the substring 'test' would be misclassified as DocExample and suppressed. Low probability but real bypass path for callers who control token values.; safe_rel_path() strips fs://workspace/ but falls back to the raw URI string when the prefix is absent (unwrap_or(resource_uri)). Non-workspace URIs (e.g. a future external-resource URI scheme) are silently accepted and treated as relative paths. This is not exploitable now (all staging artifact URIs use fs://workspace/), but could become one if a new URI scheme is added without updating the guard. A defensive explicit rejection of unknown URI schemes would be safer.; Post-commit scan runs git diff HEAD^..HEAD without error handling beyond a single if-let: if the git invocation fails silently (non-UTF8 diff, git binary missing), credentials in the commit diff are never scanned. The pre-apply scan is the primary gate so this is defense-in-depth, but the failure mode is silent.

title: v0.15.25 follow-up: fix *.md rule ordering security gap in first_matching_pattern
v0.15.25 — Auto-Approve Constitution: Rule-Based Policy + Amendment Flow
denial_reason:
Governed workflow rejected by reviewer agent.
Verdict: flag (confidence 82%)
Findings: SECURITY GAP — default rule ordering: The dual-pass glob matching in `first_matching_pattern` uses `require_literal_separator: false` for patterns without '/' (e.g., `*.md`). In this mode `*` crosses path separators, so `*.md` matches `src/auth/notes.md`. Because rule[0] (`*.md` → approve) is evaluated before rule[1] (`src/auth/**` → block), any `.md` file inside a blocked directory is auto-approved instead of blocked. No test covers this case — `auth_file_blocks` uses `src/auth/login.rs`, not a `.md` file. Fix: restrict the flat-mode pass to only match when the path itself has no separator (i.e., add `&& !path.contains('/')` to the flat-mode guard), or change `*.md` in the default block-exception to `**/*.md` and `*.md` only for the root level.; Missing test: no test for `.md` files in a directory covered by a block rule (e.g., `src/auth/notes.md`). The interaction between the `*.md` approve rule and `src/auth/**` block rule should be explicitly tested.; Pre-existing (not introduced here, recorded for completeness): `strip_uri_prefix` falls through for non-`fs://workspace/` URI schemes, passing unknown URIs as raw path strings. Currently safe because all staging artifact URIs use the workspace prefix, but a future URI scheme addition without a corresponding guard would be silently accepted.; All four v0.15.25 PLAN.md items are marked [x] and the implementation is structurally complete. The amendment flow correctly gates constitution changes behind draft review. The most-restrictive-wins aggregation across changed files is the right security default. Architecture decision to keep types in ta-policy (not ta-cli) is sound.

title: v0.15.26 follow-up: fix onclick truncation bug and split_whitespace arg parsing
v0.15.26 — Studio: Global Intent Bar + Advisor Panel with Context Tabs
denial_reason:
Governed workflow rejected by reviewer agent.
Verdict: flag (confidence 88%)
Findings: FUNCTIONAL BUG — onclick attribute truncation in renderAdvisorOptions() and renderAdvisorTab(): The pattern `onclick="handleAdvisorOption(${opt.number}, ${JSON.stringify(JSON.stringify(opt))}, ...)"` embeds a double-JSON-stringified value whose leading `"` character immediately closes the HTML attribute delimiter. The browser receives a truncated onclick value (e.g. `handleAdvisorOption(1, `) which is invalid JS syntax, so clicking advisor option buttons will silently fail or throw a parse error. Fix: use `esc(JSON.stringify(opt))` instead of `JSON.stringify(JSON.stringify(opt))` — `esc()` already HTML-encodes `"` as `"`, which keeps the attribute valid. This bug is in index.html lines ~358 and ~482.; MINOR — In execute_option() (advisor.rs, 'answer' branch): `cmd.split_whitespace().skip(1).collect()` splits on all whitespace including inside quoted strings. A command like `ta run "goal with spaces"` would produce incorrect args `["run", "\"goal", "with", "spaces\""]`. Low severity since 'answer' options currently only carry commands like `ta draft view --latest`, but fragile if broader commands are added.; SPEC DEVIATION (documented, acceptable): Phase v0.15.26 spec referenced React/TypeScript component files (`apps/ta-studio/src/components/IntentBar.tsx`, `AdvisorMenu.tsx`) that don't exist. The agent correctly identified no React build pipeline exists and implemented directly in `crates/ta-daemon/assets/index.html`. This is documented in `.ta-decisions.json` with confidence 0.95. Reviewer should confirm this architectural direction is intentional.; NOTE — The backwards-compatible `action` field in MessageResponse is now derived from the first non-clarify option in session.options. The selection logic `find(|o| o.action_type != "clarify" || session.options.len() == 1)` has an edge case: if ALL options are clarify (len > 1), the find() returns None and falls back to a separate build_legacy_action() call, running classify_intent() twice. Not a correctness issue but a minor efficiency/consistency concern.; POSITIVE — Core Rust implementation is correct: shared AdvisorSession type eliminates duplication, security level cascade (flag > config > read_only default) is sound, run_ta_command uses Command::new (no shell injection), all 40+ new tests pass per progress journal, USAGE.md fully documents the new feature.

v0.16.1.6 — Proposed .ta/plan-schema.yaml:
---
source: PLAN.md
phase_patterns:
- regex: ^##\s+Phase[\s\u{a0}]+([0-9a-z.]+)\s+[—\-]\s+(.+)$
  id_capture: phase_number
- regex: ^###\s+(v[\d.]+[a-z]?)\s+[—\-]\s+(.+)$
  id_capture: version_number
status_marker: <!--\s*status:\s*(\w+)\s*-->
statuses:
- done
- in_progress
- pending
- deferred
doc_search_dirs:
- .
- docs
- doc
- documentation
- specs
- spec
- design
- rfcs
- rfc
- planning
- plans
- requirements
- .ta
